perturbation.original_text: Company agrees that it will aggressively promote Duval and his association with Company and Company Products each Contract Year during the Contract Period.
perturbation.changed_text: Company agrees that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to promote Duval and his association with Company and Company Products each Contract Year during the Contract Period.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states 'Company agrees that it will aggressively promote Duval'. Changing it to 'commercially reasonable efforts' introduces ambiguity and weakens the obligation. 'Aggressively promote' implies a high level of commitment, while 'commercially reasonable efforts' allows for a lower standard, potentially conflicting with the intended level of marketing commitment. This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by using 2 different levels of engagement on what is expected of the company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: MARKETING COMMITMENT. Company agrees that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to promote Duval and his association with Company and Company Products each Contract Year during the Contract Period.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'aggressive promotion,' implying a high level of marketing effort. The changed text uses 'commercially reasonable efforts,' which allows for a lower level of effort. This creates a direct contradiction because the standard of promotion is explicitly different. The definition of Misaligned Terminology causing in-text contradictions when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably leading to conflicting obligations is applicable in this case, due to the different levels of commitment.

perturbation.original_text: Licensor agrees to cause Duval to wear Company's Products sold by Company when playing professional golf, and while participating in golf exhibitions and outings, upon the condition that Company supply Duval with such amounts of properly fitting Products, in fabrics and styles approved by Duval and Company, as Duval may reasonably request which are suitable for his use in tournament competition.
perturbation.changed_text: Licensor agrees to use its best endeavors to cause Duval to wear Company's Products sold by Company when playing professional golf, and while participating in golf exhibitions and outings, upon the condition that Company supply Duval with such amounts of properly fitting Products, in fabrics and styles approved by Duval, as Duval may request which are suitable for his use in tournament competition.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Licensor agrees to cause Duval' to 'Licensor agrees to use its best endeavors to cause Duval' weakens the obligation on the licensor to ensure Duval wears the products. Also, removing 'and Company' from 'fabrics and styles approved by Duval and Company' gives Duval sole approval power regarding the apparel, creating a potential contradiction regarding product selection which impacts both company's promotion and quality control. This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction in clause 8 (c) by weakening the obligation to 'best endeavors', since the company has no longer approval on the products, while the product promotion has been defined by both parts 'Duval' and 'Company' in clause 9.
perturbation.contradicted_text: COMPANY IDENTIFICATION. (a) It is agreed that the logo or name of Company (or Company's subsidiaries as mutually agreed upon) (the "Company Logo") shall be affixed to mutually agreed upon locations of Duval's Outerwear, excluding the right sleeve which is reserved for one of Licensor's other sponsors, which he wears when he plays professional golf. Company agrees that it will be responsible for, and the cost of, affixing the Company Logo on all such Outerwear. Furthermore, Company understands that if Duval participates in a special team event where there is an official uniform, then Duval is permitted to wear such uniform during such event (e.g. Ryder Cup, President's Cup, etc.).
perturbation.justification: The original text stated 'Licensor agrees to cause Duval to wear Company's Products' imposing a clear obligation. The changed text weakens this to 'Licensor agrees to use its best endeavors to cause Duval,' introducing ambiguity about the extent of the licensor's responsibility. Also, removing 'and Company' from 'fabrics and styles approved by Duval and Company' gives Duval sole approval power regarding the apparel. This creates an in-text contradiction in clause 8 (c) by weakening the obligation to 'best endeavors', since the company no longer has approval on the products. This directly conflicts with the contradicted text which states that 'the logo or name of Company shall be affixed to mutually agreed upon locations'. If Duval has sole approval power, the company has no way to ensure their logo is placed in a mutually agreed upon location, since the company can't really have a say on which products they would like to promote, or if Duval would even wear them given the 'best endeavors' modification. Therefore the changed text contradicts the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: Company agrees to protect, indemnify and save harmless Licensor, Duval and their authorized agent, or any of them, from and against any and all expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, including reasonable attorneys' fees, arising out of, or in any way connected with, actions or omissions of Company, any advertising material furnished by, or on behalf of, Company or any claim or action for personal injury, death or other cause of action involving alleged defects in Company's Products or services.
perturbation.changed_text: Company shall endeavor to protect, indemnify and save harmless Licensor, Duval and their authorized agent, or any of them, from and against reasonably foreseeable expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, including reasonable attorneys' fees, directly arising out of, or in any way connected with, gross negligence or willful misconduct of Company, any advertising material furnished by, or on behalf of, Company or any claim or action for personal injury, death or other cause of action involving alleged defects in Company's Products or services.
perturbation.explanation: The original text enforces a strict indemnification obligation on the Company for 'any and all expenses'. The modification weakens this by changing 'agrees to protect' to 'shall endeavor to protect', limiting the indemnification to 'reasonably foreseeable expenses' that 'directly' arise from 'gross negligence or willful misconduct'. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty in enforcement of the indemnification obligations. Company is weakening and limiting the term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: PRIOR APPROVAL. Company agrees that no use of the Duval Identification nor any item used in connection with the Duval Identification will be made hereunder unless and until the same has been approved by Licensor. Licensor agrees that any material, advertising or otherwise, submitted for approval as provided&sbsp;herein may be deemed by Company to have been approved hereunder if the same is not disapproved in writing within ten (10) business days after receipt thereof. Licensor agrees that any material submitted hereunder will not be unreasonably disapproved and, if it is disapproved, that Company will be advised of the specific grounds therefor. If Company desires immediate approval of advertising material hereunder, Company shall have the right to directly contact Licensor's authorized agent to obtain such approval. Company agrees to protect, indemnify and save harmless Licensor, Duval and their authorized agent, or any of them, from and against any and all expenses, damages, claims, suits, actions, judgments and costs whatsoever, arising out of, or in any way connected with, any advertising material furnished by, or on behalf of, Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates the Company's indemnification for 'any and all expenses' related to its advertising material. The modified text limits this to 'reasonably foreseeable expenses' arising from 'gross negligence or willful misconduct'. This directly contradicts the clause in the 'Contradicted Text' that obligates the Company to indemnify Licensor, Duval, etc. for 'any and all expenses' in connection with any advertising material furnished by the Company. The altered text significantly weakens the scope of indemnification, creating a direct contradiction in the obligations and rendering the enforceability of the indemnity clause uncertain.
